title: Delicate Grilled Egyptian Lamb Kebabs
description: A fresh and aerated Egyptian dish featuring Grand Marnier and miso-marinate, perfect for a special occasion. This halal-friendly recipe is cooked to perfection on a comal.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together Grand Marnier, miso paste, olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the comal over medium-high heat and thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the comal with a small amount of oil to prevent sticking, then grill the lamb kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving, then garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
Serve the kebabs with a side of couscous or rice to soak up the flavorful juices.
Garnish with additional parsley and lemon wedges, if desired.
Refrigerate any leftovers promptly and consume within 3 days.
